meaning: adj.(个子)矮小的,树丛繁盛的,多短硬毛的
examples: One-storey houses erected in the late 1940s and early 1950s sit behind scrubby gardens.
In 1770 Captain Cook had been unimpressed by the scrubby, sandy bluffs visible from his ship.
Following into a hole of the base of some scrubby bushes, he started digging with a metal pole.
"Get your kitsch on Route 66" might be a better slogan for Arizona's scrubby stretch of Mother Road.
Fire is the cheapest and fastest way to clear the ground of misshaped and scrubby trees, together with undergrowth.
